pattern: A greedy cashier tracks only its count of fives and tens, always handing back the largest bills first so scarce fives are conserved for when they are the only valid change.
hints: You only ever need to give back fives and tens, so keeping a running count of each denomination you hold is enough.
A twenty can be settled either with a ten plus a five or with three fives; preferring to release a ten first keeps your flexible fives in reserve.

Walk the bills keeping counts of fives and tens: a five adds a five, a ten needs one five back, a twenty takes a ten and a five if possible else three fives; if you ever cannot make change return false.

edge_inputs.description: first example: change is always available
second example: not enough fives when a twenty arrives
first customer pays ten with no fives on hand
twenty settled with three fives rather than a ten
greedy must spend the ten, not three fives, to survive a later twenty

stress.description: long random queue of fives, tens, and twenties
stress.note: mostly fails partway, exercising the early-exit path
